title: Boat Rental in Bora Bora: A Complete Guide for 2026
html:
Planning a boat rental in Bora Bora? Rates start around €200 for a half-day small motorboat from Vaitape harbour, and you can browse verified options on BoatHire24 before you book.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Bora Bora?

Prices range from €80 for a shared tour to €1,500 for a full-day crewed yacht.

  • Shared lagoon tour: from €80 per person for 2, 3 hours with snorkelling stops.
  • Small motorboat (4.5, 5 m) half-day: €200, €350 without fuel, self-drive.
  • Catamaran (38, 45 ft) full-day: €800, €1,200 including skipper.
  • Crewed luxury yacht (50+ ft) full-day: €1,200, €1,500 with crew and lunch included.

Where to set off in Bora Bora

The main departure point is Vaitape harbour on the west coast, where several rental operators are based. Other starting points include the Bora Bora Yacht Club at Motu Mute, the marina at Sofitel Bora Bora Marara Beach Resort, the public jetty at Anau, and the boat ramp near the InterContinental Le Moana. Most rentals include a briefing on local navigation and the lagoon’s channels. The pass (Teavanui passage) is the only deep-water entrance to the lagoon and requires caution for first-timers.

Which boat to rent

  1. Small motorboat (e.g. Olympic 490 Rhodes), ideal for couples exploring the lagoon at your own pace; up to 4 guests; from €250 half-day. See the Olympic 490 listing on BoatHire24 for details.
  2. Catamaran (Lagoon 450 or similar), spacious cruising for families or two couples; 8, 10 guests; from €900 full-day.
  3. Luxury motor yacht (Azimut 55), crewed charter with all amenities; up to 10 guests; from €1,500 full-day.
  4. Jet ski or e-foil, high-speed adventure for thrill-seekers; 1, 2 guests; from €150 per hour.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Bora Bora?

Yes, for self-drive boats over 5hp you need an ICC or equivalent French licence, or proof of experience.

If you don't hold a licence, skipper options are widely available, many boats can be chartered with a local captain who knows the lagoon’s currents and best snorkelling spots.

The high season runs from June to October (dry winter months) when the trade winds blow reliably from the east. November to April brings higher humidity and occasional rain, but also lower rates. Always check with the operator whether a licence is required for the specific boat; most providers on BoatHire24 clearly state their requirements. For a wide selection of vessels, yacht charter Bora Bora on the platform to compare verified listings.

At a glance

  • Price range: €80, €1,500 per trip (shared to crewed yacht)
  • Peak months: June, October
  • Typical trip length: half-day (3, 4 h) or full-day (8 h)
  • Max guests: 2, 12 depending on boat type
  • Currency: EUR widely accepted; prices quoted in EUR

Questions about boat rental in Bora Bora

Can I rent a boat in Bora Bora without a licence?

Yes, you can hire a skipper-driven boat, which is recommended for first-time visitors to avoid navigational hazards in the lagoon.

What is the best time to rent a boat in Bora Bora?

The best time is from June to October when the weather is dry, seas are calm, and visibility for snorkelling is excellent.

How far is it from Vaitape to the main coral gardens?

The distance to the famous coral gardens near Motu Tapu is about 4 nautical miles, a 20-minute boat ride from Vaitape harbour.

Are there fuel stations on the lagoon?

Fuel is available at Vaitape harbour and at the Bora Bora Yacht Club; most rentals require you to refill before returning the boat.

Whether you are a first-time visitor or a seasoned mariner, renting a boat in Bora Bora is the best way to experience the lagoon’s turquoise waters and dramatic peaks. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Bora Bora or list your own. For owners wishing to tap into the global cruising community, list your boat on the platform and start earning income in 2026.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Bora Bora and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
